corrections.note: Name in list misread; image clearly has Ιωδιάσουβος.
Transcript has a substantially wrong phrase; printed text reads γὰρ ἐπιδόθησεν ἐνώπιον πάντων across the line break, commonly normalized here as one span.

corrections.current: Καὶ εἶπεν Ἀτταράτης Ἔσρα τῷ ἀρχιερεῖ καὶ καὶ ἀναγνώστῃ καὶ τοῖς Λευείταις τοῖς διδάσκουσιν τὸ πλῆθος ἐπὶ πᾶσι τοῖς 50 Ἡ ἡμέρα αὕτη ἐστὶν ἁγία
corrections.correct: Καὶ εἶπεν Ἀτταράτης Ἔσρα τῷ ἀρχιερεῖ καὶ ἀναγνώστῃ καὶ τοῖς Λευείταις τοῖς διδάσκουσιν τὸ πλῆθος ἐπὶ πᾶσιν ὅτι Ἡ ἡμέρα αὕτη ἐστὶν ἁγία
corrections.note: Dropped final -ν in πᾶσιν, omitted ὅτι, and duplicated καὶ.
Verb form in image is present δοξάζει, not future δοξάσει.
One extra repeated word πάντα in transcript.
Lemma in apparatus is singular εστησεν, matching printed apparatus.

corrections.current: ευλογησεν Α | Αζαριας] Ἔσρας Α | υψιστω] pr κω θω τω Α
corrections.correct: ηυλογησεν Α | Αζαριας] Ἔσρας Α | υψιστω] pr κω θω τω Α
corrections.note: Printed apparatus shows ηυλογησεν with initial eta.
Latin abbreviation in apparatus is improbab, not improbv.
No change to content; retained because surrounding apparatus checked. Main issue on this line is elsewhere.
Transcript imported body/apparatus confusion; printed apparatus reads Αβραιας A here, with Σαββαταιας belonging to the previous variant sequence.
Checked as printed; no correction beyond neighboring items.

corrections.current: om και προς το πλ. ανεγ. τον ν. του κυριου Α
corrections.correct: om και προς το πλ. ανεγινωσκον τον ν. του κυριου Α
corrections.note: Abbreviation expanded incorrectly; printed apparatus has ανεγινωσκον, not ανεγ.
Apparatus has nomen sacrum δς without preceding article.

uncertain.note: The exact printed form appears to be γὰρ ἐπιδόθησεν rather than the transcript's προηλῆλθεν...; image is clear enough for correction above, but the initial letters should be rechecked if diplomatic exactness is required.

notes: Running head matches. Body contains several substantive errors in the middle section and one duplicated word. Apparatus mostly matches but has a few misread names/Latin abbreviations and one nomen sacrum issue.
